$comment: TF-C50 (D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH + D-ML7-2.9): a DIRECT call to an undefined extern function. In an arm64 RELOCATABLE .o this lowers to a plain BL + R_AARCH64_CALL26 (foreign linker inserts the veneer) with the dead callee-address lea suppressed -- witnessed structurally by test_elf_writer's ObjectExternCallEmitsUndefImportNameAndCall26RelocOnAarch64 pin + the foreign gcc-PIE/qemu probe. THIS runnable example proves the direct extern call RUNS end-to-end on each shipped target: abs(argc-43) with argc==1 -> abs(-42) == 42 -> exit 42. `argc` (runtime) blocks const-folding so the `release` arm exercises the real BL/CALL, not a folded literal. RED-ON-DISABLE: a mis-materialized callee makes the call land elsewhere -> not 42.
